prose: Fukui reactivity indices for benzaldehyde were computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level of theory in PySCF, in the gas phase, with neutral charge and multiplicity 1. The calculation employed a single-point evaluation on the user-supplied geometry to compute the condensed Fukui indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor (ΔƒD) using Mulliken partial charges. Three electronic states were evaluated: neutral (N), cation (N−1, multiplicity 2), and anion (N+1, multiplicity 2). All calculations converged successfully. The most electrophilic site is the aromatic carbon ortho to the aldehyde (C-3, f⁺ = 0.3994; dual descriptor ΔƒD = 0.3626), and the most nucleophilic site is the carbonyl oxygen (f⁻ = 0.2384). Integrity checks confirmed charge conservation for both f⁺ and f⁻ (sum = 1.0000). No warnings reported. The result is trustworthy.
